CREATE TABLE contingency_test (id UInt32, a UInt32, b UInt32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT contingency(a, b) FROM (SELECT number % 10 AS a, number % 4 AS b FROM numbers(100));
SELECT a, b, contingency(a, b) FROM (SELECT number % 10 AS a, number % 4 AS b FROM numbers(150)) GROUP BY a, b;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS count, contingency(a, b) FROM (SELECT number % 10 AS a, number % 3 AS b FROM numbers(200)) GROUP BY a, b HAVING count > 1;
SELECT contingency(sub.a, sub.b) FROM (SELECT number % 10 AS a, number % 4 AS b FROM numbers(150) GROUP BY a, b) AS sub;
WITH (SELECT max(a) FROM contingency_test) AS max_a, (SELECT min(a) FROM contingency_test) AS min_a SELECT contingency(max_a, min_a) AS boundary_contingency;
CREATE TABLE contingency_join (id UInt32, c UInt32, d UInt32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
SELECT contingency(ct.a, cj.d) FROM contingency_test ct JOIN contingency_join cj ON ct.a = cj.c;
SELECT contingency(a, b) FROM (SELECT id, number % 10 AS a FROM numbers(100) WHERE number < 50) AS t1 CROSS JOIN (SELECT id, number % 5 AS b FROM numbers(100) WHERE number >= 50) AS t2;
